id.description: Unique identifier for the Ticket entity.

phone.description: Phone number for the delivery contact.

googleMapsLink.description: Google Maps link for the delivery location.

status.description: Status of the ticket (e.g., Pending, Prepared, En Route, Delivered).

ownerId.description: Reference to User who owns the delivery. (Relationship: User 1:N Ticket)

customerId.description: Reference to Customer who is receiving delivery. (Relationship: Customer 1:N Ticket)

deliveryPriority.description: Priority of the delivery route optimization.

completionDate.description: Date when the ticket was completed.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

roles.description: Roles of the user (e.g., Driver, Admin).

avatarColor.description: The hex color code for the user's avatar fallback.

Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: Represents a customer receiving a delivery.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Customer entity.

definition.description: Stores user profiles. Access controlled via path-based rules. Only the user or an admin can read/write.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores all tickets in a single root collection. Access is controlled by checking ownerId, driverId, or user role within security rules.

params.name: ticketId
params.description: The unique identifier for the ticket.

definition.description: Stores customer information.  If collaboration is needed, consider adding a 'members' map and denormalizing.

params.name: customerId
params.description: The unique identifier for the customer.

definition.description: Indicates admin role membership. Existence implies admin rights.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the admin user.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to support the DecoEntrega application, focusing on secure and efficient data management for tickets, users, and customers. The structure leverages a root-level 'tickets' collection to allow for easier querying by admins and vendors, while still using security rules to enforce data access based on ownership and roles.

**Users Collection:** Stores user profiles. Access is primarily managed through path-based rules ( `/users/{userId}`), ensuring only the user or admins can access user-specific data.  Authentication relies on Firebase Auth, with user roles stored directly in the user document. Admins can be managed via a dedicated `/roles_admin/{userId}` collection, checked for existence only.

**Tickets Collection:** All tickets are stored in a single root-level collection (`/tickets/{ticketId}`). This simplifies global queries (e.g., for dashboards or history pages for admins/vendors). Security is enforced by rules that check the `ownerId` or `driverId` fields within the ticket document against the authenticated user's ID.

**Customers Collection:** Stores customer information.  Customers are global and not user-specific.  Tickets store a `customerId` referencing a customer.

**QAPs (Queries and Permissions):**
*   **Secure Lists:** Queries on the root `tickets` collection can be secured by using `where()` clauses that match the user's ID, which Firestore rules can then validate (e.g., `allow list: if request.query.where.driverId == request.auth.uid`). For admins/vendors, rules can allow broader access based on their role.
*   **Authorization Independence:**  Denormalizing data like `ownerId` and `driverId` into the ticket document is now even more critical as it's the primary mechanism for authorization checks.
*   **Data Integrity:**  The structure supports enforcing invariants such as ownership (via `ownerId`), timestamps (created/updated), and denormalized data consistency.
